name: Tesla Model 3 (Sentry Mode)
whySpecs: Sentry mode continuously overwrites old footage. Standard cards fail quickly. You need High Endurance cards rated for 10,000+ write/erase cycles.

faq.q: Why do dash cam cards fail so fast?
faq.a: Dash cams continuously overwrite data. Standard cards are optimized for intermittent writes. High Endurance cards are rated for 10,000+ write/erase cycles, making them 10-20x more durable for this use case.

faq.q: Do I need a special card for Tesla Sentry Mode?
faq.a: Yes. Sentry Mode records 24/7 when parked. Standard cards typically fail within weeks. Use High Endurance (TBW 10,000+) or Surveillance-class cards only.

faq.q: What's the best capacity for Sentry Mode?
faq.a: 256GB is ideal. Tesla Sentry records in clips. 256GB holds approximately 5-7 days of continuous footage depending on quality. Larger capacities reduce how often you need to manage the card.

notes: Premium EV. Requires High Endurance cards for reliable dash cam operation.

name: Generic 4K Dash Cam
whySpecs: 4K dash cams write at ~50-100 Mbps bitrate depending on resolution and codec. V30 ensures 30MB/s sustained write speed, preventing dropped frames. High Endurance cards extend lifespan from months to years.

faq.q: What's the difference between dash cam and regular microSD cards?
faq.a: Dash cam cards are High Endurance rated (10,000-40,000 TBW). Regular cards are rated for 500-1,000 TBW. Dash cams loop-record continuously, causing 10-20x more wear per year.

faq.q: Can I use a regular fast card?
faq.a: Not recommended long-term. High-speed UHS-II cards (like Extreme Pro) will work initially but fail within 2-4 months of continuous recording. High Endurance cards last 1-2+ years.
64GB stores 2-4 hours (good for daily commute). 128GB stores 4-8 hours (covers overnight parking + commute). For Sentry Mode or 24/7 recording, use 256GB+.

notes: Universal dash cam recommendation. Works with Rexing, Viofo, Garmin, VANTRUE, and most aftermarket 4K cameras.

name: Viofo A229 Pro Dual Dash Cam
whySpecs: Viofo A229 Pro is a dual-channel 4K dash cam (front + interior). Each channel writes continuously at ~50 Mbps. You need TWO cards minimum (one for each channel). High Endurance essential.

faq.q: Do I need two cards for the A229 Pro?
faq.a: Yes, absolutely. The A229 Pro has two SD card slots (one for front, one for interior). You need two separate High Endurance cards.
128GB per card minimum for balanced storage. 256GB gives you 6-8 hours per card (front = 6-8 hours, interior = 6-8 hours, staggered). Dual 256GB = comprehensive coverage.
No. The A229 Pro writes to both cards simultaneously at high bitrate. Regular cards fail within weeks. Use V30 High Endurance only.

notes: Professional dual-channel dash cam. Requires dual High Endurance cards for reliable operation.

name: Viofo T130 Pro
searchTerms: best sd card for viofo t130 pro
whySpecs: Viofo specifically restricts the T130 Pro to UHS-I U3-rated, dashcam/high-endurance microSD cards — not general-purpose consumer cards. The camera loop-records continuously, overwriting old footage in a cycle that wears out standard cards within weeks in a hot windshield environment. Viofo caps supported capacity at 256GB, with 128GB called out as the preferable, more reliable size.

faq.q: Can I use a regular fast microSD card in the T130 Pro?
faq.a: No — Viofo's own guidance restricts the T130 Pro to U3-rated, dashcam/high-endurance cards. Standard consumer cards aren't built for continuous loop-overwrite recording and tend to fail within weeks of dash cam use, especially in hot windshield-mounted conditions.

faq.q: What's the maximum card size for the T130 Pro?
faq.a: 256GB is the maximum supported capacity, with 128GB called out as preferable for reliability. Bigger isn't automatically better here — Viofo's guidance favors a properly rated endurance card over maxing out capacity.

faq.q: Why do dash cam cards need to be 'high-endurance'?
faq.a: The T130 Pro continuously overwrites old footage in a loop, which wears out standard consumer microSD cards far faster than normal photo/video use. High-endurance cards are built with this write/erase cycle pattern in mind and last years instead of weeks.

notes: Restrict affiliate output strictly to High-Endurance/U3 cards rated for continuous overwrite loops. Explicitly warn against standard consumer cards — heat + loop-recording wear kills them early in hot windshields. Per Viofo's own guidance, max 256GB, 128GB preferable.

name: Nextbase iQ 4K
searchTerms: best sd card for nextbase iq
whySpecs: The Nextbase iQ is built around continuous loop recording with cloud/AI features layered on top, which pushes the camera toward higher-bitrate 4K capture than a typical dash cam. Nextbase's own guidance recommends U3-rated dashcam-compatible cards, calling out Nextbase-branded or SanDisk High Endurance (U3) cards specifically — with periodic reformatting recommended to manage wear from the continuous overwrite loop.

faq.q: What SD card does Nextbase recommend for the iQ 4K?
faq.a: Nextbase's guidance points to U3-rated dashcam-compatible cards, specifically calling out Nextbase-branded cards or SanDisk High Endurance (U3) as preferred options. General-purpose consumer cards aren't built for the iQ's continuous loop-overwrite recording.

faq.q: What capacity should I get for the iQ 4K's cloud/AI features?
faq.a: 256GB-512GB is recommended. The iQ's cloud/AI features push higher-bitrate 4K capture than a typical dash cam, so capacity guidance skews larger here than on simpler dashcam models.

faq.q: Do I need to do anything to maintain the card over time?
faq.a: Yes — Nextbase recommends periodically reformatting the card. Continuous loop recording wears down even high-endurance cards over time, and a periodic reformat helps maintain reliable write performance.

notes: Same High-Endurance-only framing as T130 Pro. Nextbase's cloud/AI features push higher bitrate 4K, so capacity guidance skews larger (256GB+) alongside the endurance requirement. No authoritative Nextbase source found stating a specific write-class floor (e.g. V30) for this model — copy is tied to U3 + dashcam/high-endurance rather than asserting V30.
